verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (hK2) is a secreted serine protease of the kallikrein family. It is synthesized as a preproprotein with a signal peptide directing it into the secretory pathway, where the signal peptide and propeptide are cleaved, and the mature enzyme is constitutively secreted into extracellular fluids (seminal plasma, serum). It has no transmembrane domain, no GPI anchor, and no documented stable covalent or wash-resistant tethering to a cell-surface TM partner. Its detection in serum/seminal plasma as a soluble protease marks it as secreted-only. No evidence for cell-state-induced surface display or lysosomal exocytosis pathway.
